news.summary: Arthur Hayes has provided a detailed argument suggesting that the U.S. government, under Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, is increasingly relying on stablecoins as a financial tool to support growing federal deficits and control bond yields. Hayes argues that rather than investing in traditional financial technology companies like Circle (NYSE: CRCL), the key is recognizing that the government has given Too Big To Fail (TBTF) banks access to a multi-trillion-dollar reservoir of liquidity disguised as innovation. He predicts that the adoption of stablecoins by TBTF banks, along with regulatory perks such as Supplemental Leverage Ratio (SLR) exemptions and the potential discontinuation of the Federal Reserve’s interest payments on reserve balances, could lead to a surge in demand for Treasury bills. According to Hayes, this could result in the release of $6.8 trillion in T-bill buying power through stablecoin-powered bank deposits, with an additional $3.3 trillion potentially being unleashed. For further details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Arthur Hayes believes stablecoins are key to funding federal deficits. $6.8 trillion in T-bill buying power through bank deposits.

news.summary: In Dublin, the database "Netherlands Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio" is now available through ResearchAndMarkets.com. The data shows that the existing IT power capacity in the Netherlands surpasses the upcoming data center capacity by over double, highlighting a robust current infrastructure. Digital Realty and Equinix maintain their positions as the top data center operators in the Netherlands in terms of total white/raised floor area and IT power. The upcoming data center capacity is primarily concentrated in Amsterdam, showcasing a major growth trend in the region.
news.headline: Netherlands data center market booming with existing IT power capacity more than twice upcoming capacity. Amsterdam a key focus. Digital Realty and Equinix lead operators.

news.summary: Clearmind Medicine Inc. (Nasdaq: CMND), a biotech company based in Vancouver, announced today that it has obtained Institutional Review Board (IRB) approval from Tel Aviv Sourasky Medical Center (TASMC) in Israel for its Phase 1/2a clinical trial of CMND-100. This novel psychedelic-derived drug, which is MEAI-based, aims to address Alcohol Use Disorder (AUD). TASMC, a top clinical site in Israel, will now be able to enroll patients for this groundbreaking trial before it officially begins.
news.headline: Clearmind Medicine received IRB approval for Phase 1/2a trial at TASMC in Tel Aviv for CMND-100, to treat Alcohol Use Disorder.

news.summary: Manta Network has joined forces with Wello to incorporate fiat payment options into SUPERFORTUNE, aiming to improve user accessibility to Web3 applications by facilitating smooth fiat transactions. news.summary: It typically takes several weeks for bone fractures and breaks to heal, with an average healing time ranging from a few weeks to several months depending on the severity of the injury. In an effort to expedite this process, a natural remedy called CALCEED has been developed by SYNEVIT®. This optimized calcium dietary supplement is designed to accelerate the healing of bone fractures and breaks. This innovation comes as a promising solution to the lengthy recovery period associated with such injuries.
news.headline: CALCEED® accelerates bone healing. SYNEVIT®'s supplement shortens long recovery times for fractures.

news.summary: In San Francisco, Reddit, Inc. (NYSE: RDDT) shared that their financial results for the second quarter of 2025 will be disclosed post-market closure on Thursday, July 31, 2025. A conference call to review the results is scheduled for 2 p.m. PT / 5 p.m. ET on the same day. To access the live webcast of the call and relevant earnings documents, individuals can visit Reddit's Investor Relations webpage at investor.redditinc.com and the investor relations subreddit r/RDDT. A playback of the conference call will also be available.
news.headline: Reddit will announce Q2 2025 financial results on July 31, 2025, with a conference call at 2 p.m. PT / 5 p.m. ET. Webcast available on investor.redditinc.com and r/RDDT.

news.summary: The robotic surgery market is experiencing rapid growth, expected to reach USD 33.8 billion by 2029, with a CAGR of 16.1%. This growth is fueled by technological advancements, healthcare automation, and the global need to reduce clinical errors. Robotic surgery is redefining surgical precision and patient recovery, particularly in soft tissue procedures. These robots offer increased accuracy, faster patient turnaround, and reduced complications. Robotic surgery machines are also solving medication errors and procedural inefficiencies, transforming care delivery. Emerging markets in countries like India, China, Brazil, and South Africa are driving the growth of robotic surgery, with rising surgical volumes and investor-friendly regulations.
news.headline: Robotic surgery market to reach $33.8 billion by 2029, growing at 16.1% CAGR. Surge in demand for precision and patient recovery driving this transformation in healthcare. East and South emerging as investable markets.
